diagnoses.name: End-stage renal disease
diagnoses.evidence_span: Patient is a long-term dialysis-dependent individual with end-stage renal disease

diagnoses.name: Type 2 diabetes mellitus with diabetic chronic kidney disease
diagnoses.evidence_span: type 2 diabetes mellitus complicated by diabetic nephropathy and diabetic chronic kidney disease

diagnoses.name: Hypertensive chronic kidney disease with stage 5 CKD
diagnoses.evidence_span: hypertensive chronic kidney disease with stage 5 CKD

diagnoses.name: Diabetic nephropathy
diagnoses.evidence_span: type 2 diabetes mellitus complicated by diabetic nephropathy

diagnoses.name: Sepsis due to Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus
diagnoses.evidence_span: Cultures subsequently grew Methicillin-resistant Staphylococcus aureus (MRSA), confirming sepsis

diagnoses.name: Acute and subacute infective endocarditis
diagnoses.evidence_span: Transesophageal echocardiography revealed vegetations consistent with acute and subacute infective endocarditis

diagnoses.name: Dependence on renal dialysis
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient has been dialyzing for several years due to dependence on renal dialysis

diagnoses.name: Hyperkalemia
diagnoses.evidence_span: Hyperkalemia was managed aggressively with calcium gluconate, dextrose with regular insulin, and sodium polystyrene sulfonate

diagnoses.name: Anemia of chronic kidney disease
diagnoses.evidence_span: Anemia of chronic kidney disease was monitored; supplementation with calcitriol and vitamin D was continued

diagnoses.name: Constipation
diagnoses.evidence_span: The patient's constipation, a chronic issue exacerbated by medications and dietary restrictions of end-stage renal disease

diagnoses.name: Acute fluid overload
diagnoses.evidence_span: the patient required intermittent urinary filtration for less than 6 hours per day given the acute fluid overload state superimposed on chronic kidney disease

diagnoses.name: Infection and inflammatory reaction due to cardiac infusion device
diagnoses.evidence_span: An infection and inflammatory reaction due to the cardiac infusion device was identified

procedures.name: Removal of external cardiac infusion device
procedures.evidence_span: The patient underwent removal of the external cardiac infusion device given the infected implant

procedures.name: Insertion of new tunneled vascular access device
procedures.evidence_span: a new tunneled vascular access device was inserted into the chest subcutaneous tissue via percutaneous approach

procedures.name: Placement of infusion device into right atrium
procedures.evidence_span: An infusion device was placed into the right atrium percutaneously for continued medication administration

procedures.name: Removal of old tunneled vascular access device from trunk
procedures.evidence_span: The old tunneled vascular access device from the trunk was removed using external approach

procedures.name: Transesophageal echocardiography
procedures.evidence_span: Transesophageal echocardiography revealed vegetations consistent with acute and subacute infective endocarditis

procedures.name: Intermittent urinary filtration
procedures.evidence_span: the patient required intermittent urinary filtration for less than 6 hours per day

medications.name: Vancomycin
medications.evidence_span: Vancomycin 1000 mg IV (continue per ID recommendations)

medications.name: Furosemide
medications.dosage: 20 mg PO daily and 40-100 mg IV
medications.evidence_span: Furosemide 20 mg PO daily and 40-100 mg IV post-dialysis as needed

medications.name: Calcium carbonate
medications.evidence_span: Calcium carbonate 500 mg PO with meals

medications.name: Sevelamer carbonate
medications.evidence_span: Sevelamer carbonate 800 mg PO with meals

medications.name: Senna
medications.evidence_span: Senna 8.6 mg PO daily as needed

medications.name: Insulin
medications.evidence_span: Insulin per home regimen (6 and 8 units SC as previously established)

medications.name: Sodium polystyrene sulfonate
medications.evidence_span: Sodium polystyrene sulfonate 30 gm PO as needed for hyperkalemia

medications.name: Acetaminophen
medications.evidence_span: Acetaminophen 650 mg PO as needed for pain

medications.name: Calcium gluconate
medications.evidence_span: Hyperkalemia was managed aggressively with calcium gluconate, dextrose with regular insulin, and sodium polystyrene sulfonate

medications.name: Dextrose with regular insulin
medications.evidence_span: Hyperkalemia was managed aggressively with calcium gluconate, dextrose with regular insulin, and sodium polystyrene sulfonate
